title: NOC 42200 – Paralegals and Related Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Paralegals and Related Occupations under NOC 42200 are legal paraprofessionals who support lawyers, courts, tribunals, government offices, and private clients in a wide range of legal matters. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation requires formal legal education, licensing (in many provinces), and specialized training. Professionals in this category work in law firms, courts, corporate legal departments, government agencies, and independent legal practices.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group422 – Paraprofessional occupations in legal, social, community, and education servicesMinor Group4220 – Paraprofessional occupationsNOC Code42200

duties: Research court files and legal records
Assist with mediation and dispute resolution
Represent clients in small claims court
Represent clients before tribunals and administrative bodies
Represent clients before the Trade Marks Opposition Board
employment_requirements: Provincial license required for advanced legal services
Completion of a community college paralegal program or
Legal or justice system background required
University degree in law or related field
In British Columbia: membership in the Society of Notaries Public required
Two years of trademark law experience
salary_prospects: $34,700 – $88,600 per year,depending on specialization, province, and experience.
Senior legal clerks, trademark agents, and specialized professionals may earn significantly higher salaries.
job_outlook: Real estate and intellectual property market growth
labour_market_demand: Growth in small claims and tribunal matters
Rising real estate and intellectual property transactions
Opportunities in public and private sectors
Career progression to senior legal clerk or specialized legal consultant
Strong employment stability in growing legal markets
Paralegals are especially in demand in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia.
